patch.value: GH-527: semantic staleness warning recommends 'pm reindex' which is absent from the base install

GH-527 (2026-07-12, repro with --no-extensions on 2026.7.12): pm search --semantic on a stale index prints 'run pm reindex --mode hybrid', but reindex ships only in optional @unbrained/pm-search-advanced — on a base install the advertised remediation fails with Unknown command reindex, a guaranteed failed-retry loop for agents following printed guidance verbatim. Fix direction: make remediation hints capability-aware — render hints only for commands resolvable in the current runtime configuration, else render the install step first (GH proposal options 1/3; option 2 = shipping a thin base reindex is a scope decision, not required). CLASS: rendered hints must be generated from actual runtime capability/contract state, same family as the pm-mpbb ledger hint-correctness entries (pm-ulqu enum value-domain hints, pm-gdi7 incomplete flag-hint list, pm-b24b platform-correct paths) — audit other package-owned-command hints (calendar/templates/guide/dedupe-audit etc.) at fix time for the same trap.

The staleness warning renders a remediation that is executable in the CURRENT runtime configuration: when @unbrained/pm-search-advanced is absent it says to pm install the package first (or points to the actual base reindex path), when present it keeps the pm reindex --mode hybrid hint; no rendered hint on a base install leads to Unknown command; spec covers both configurations (--no-extensions and with the package)
